new_value: Pixie double over down enters a double over down through a pixie set. Pixie set the bag about waist high and plant the foot as soon as possible; as the bag starts to descend, begin exactly as you would for a double over down.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Parkwalks and double over downs are the strongest groundwork, since the move is essentially a double over down off a pixie entry. Working it on both sides from the start pays off even before the flip is fast.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A waist-high pixie set and a solid double over down are the two halves. The new element is launching the double over down straight out of the pixie set.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pixie symposium mirage begins like a pixie same-side clipper, but as the bag passes over the planted leg you jump over both the bag and your other foot, catching it on the inside of that other foot.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The defining moment is the jump over the bag and the trailing foot, which redirects the catch to the inside of the far foot. Reading when the bag clears the planted leg is what cues the jump.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A pixie set and comfort with same-side clipper positioning are the starting point. The new skill is the jump over the bag and far foot into an inside catch.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pixie whirl enters a whirl from a pixie set. Start the pixie set a little lower than waist high, and just before the first dex foot touches the ground, jump from the rooted foot into a whirling motion.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: An impressive pixie set is what makes this one look good, so building it through moves like smear, dimwalk, and magellan helps, on both sides. Drilling dimwalk, paradox whirl, and symposium whirl rounds out the pieces the whirl entry needs.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A strong pixie set and a whirl are the two halves. The timing element is launching into the whirling motion just before the first dex foot lands.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Smog pairs a clean pixie set with a quick double leg over. Set the pixie a little above waist height and keep it clean by executing the dex at the peak of the bag's rise and turning the hips, throwing the knee so the lower leg and foot follow it clear of the bag; once the pixie is done, plant the pixie foot as fast as you can and go straight into the double leg over.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Two elements have to be solid: a clean, straight pixie set and a smooth, fast double leg over. Turning the hips and throwing the knee on the set is what keeps the bag travelling straight up and down.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A clean pixie set and a reliable double leg over are the two halves. The move asks for both to be quick and tidy so the double leg over has time to finish.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Spinning paradox blender sets chest high and tight to the body, with a feel close to spinning osis. Begin the spin as soon as the bag leaves your foot and get it finished so you are no longer turning when the blender starts; after the spin, spot the bag and execute the blender.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Getting the spin done early and cleanly is the key, so the blender is not rushed against a body still rotating. Setting tight to the body keeps the bag where you can spot it as you come around.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A paradox blender and comfort spinning are the foundations; the entry feels much like spinning osis. The demand it adds is finishing the spin before the blender begins.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Spinning paradox mirage buys time with a tall set. Set the bag at least chest high and straight up, get the spin over with as soon as the bag leaves the foot, then spot the bag as you come around and drive the dexing leg quickly, finishing the dex as the spin completes so there is plenty of time for the toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A tall, straight set is what gives you room for both the spin and the dex. Spot the bag the instant you come around and start the dex immediately rather than waiting.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A paradox mirage and comfort spinning are the two halves. The move's demand is timing: finish the spin early, then finish the dex as the spin ends.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Stepping butterfly steps the bag back under the leg before a butterfly. Start as for a butterfly with your upper-body weight above your hips; jump up and backward off the plant leg at the exact moment you set the bag, raising the dexing leg to clear it as you jump back, then once both legs land, simply hop forward into the butterfly. It works with a very low set.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Sidewalk is the easiest move for learning the stepping set, so it is worth trying first. The timing hinge is jumping back off the plant leg exactly as you set, then hopping forward for the butterfly.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A butterfly and the stepping set are the two pieces, and sidewalk is good preparation for the set. The new coordination is the backward jump on the set and the forward hop into the butterfly.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Stepping ducking paradox blender adds a duck of the head to the stepping paradox blender, and the duck carries a strong pivot. As you duck, pivot the body almost like a half spin to bring yourself around with the bag.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The pivot is the part to get right: ducking without turning leaves you out of position, so treat the duck and a half-turn pivot as one motion.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with the stepping paradox blender and with ducking the head are the starting points. The new element is the pivoting half-turn folded into the duck.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Stepping paradox torque builds on a paradox torque with a stepping entry. Set so the height and timing of the first two dexes feel like a fog, and consider taking the move pogo style, which can save time getting to the second dex.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: It rests on several prerequisite skills, so a consistent paradox torque, where you understand when and how much to crank, is essential. Schooling fogs helps because their set height and early timing feel very similar, and it is worth experimenting with set height, since the workable window is bigger than expected.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A consistent paradox torque and comfortable fogs are the groundwork. The move's own demands are the stepping entry, optional pogo timing, and dialing in a set height that leaves room for the dexes.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Symposium eggbeater starts with the same motion as a flail, then adds the leg over of an eggbeater. Begin exactly as you would for a flail, but jump a little higher to give yourself room to complete the remaining leg over dex.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Hitting flails consistently matters most, since the opening motion is identical; the extra height on the jump is what makes space for the eggbeater finish. Strong eggbeaters round out the second half.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A consistent flail and a solid eggbeater are the two halves. The new element is jumping higher off the flail motion to fit in the leg over.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Symposium torque uses a small hop and upper-body rotation to power the dex. Take a small hop while setting the bag to help with the initial jump for the dexterity, and use your upper body, especially the arms, to drive the spin.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The hop on the set and an active upper body are what make the spin manageable. Experience with other symposium tricks like PS whirl and flail helps, since the move needs comparable leg strength.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with other symposium moves and the leg strength they build are the foundation. The specific techniques to bring are the small setting hop and using the arms to spin.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Toe clipper feels much like a knee clipper but catches on the toe. Just before you jump, bring one leg across your body to clear it out of the way of the kicking leg, then jump and bring the kicking leg's knee up so the toe is lifted into the bag by the rest of the leg, landing on the same foot you jumped with.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Getting the support leg across and out of the way is the enabling move, just as in knee clipper. Practicing on both sides from the start keeps the move balanced.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with the crossed-leg clipper position, as in knee clipper, is the main prerequisite. The new piece is lifting the toe into the bag with the knee drive and landing on the jumping foot.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Double spin carries two full rotations under the bag. Lead the rotation with the top of your body and let the lower body follow, the way a skateboarder sequences a big spin, so the turn builds smoothly through the two revolutions.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Driving the upper body first and letting the legs follow is what makes the double rotation come around in time. Build up to it from a single spin once that sequencing feels natural.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A clean single spin is the prerequisite. The move's demand is generating enough rotation by leading with the upper body to complete two turns before the catch.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description and community tips into structured instructional prose.

old_value: A whirl delay followed by a swirl (legacy sequential description).
new_value: Whirling Swirl blends a whirl and a swirl into one coordinated movement. The two motions happen together, not in sequence: as the leg carries the bag around, the whirl and the swirl are woven into a single flight that lands back in a clipper. A less common alternate name, Swirling Whirl, emphasizes the same movement from the opposite perspective; it describes the same action, not a different order.
source_note: Editorial: replaced the legacy sequential description with an integrated-movement About and noted Swirling Whirl as the less common alternate name.

new_value: Swirling Whirl is a less commonly used name for the same integrated whirl-and-swirl movement. It emphasizes the whirl rather than the swirl, but does not describe a different sequence of actions. Most players know this trick as Whirling Swirl.
source_note: Editorial: authored an About for the alternate name that steers to the canonical Whirling Swirl and removes sequential implication.

new_value: From a clipper, a whirling motion of the opposite leg and a swirl of the setting foot are blended into one continuous action, landing back on the other leg in a clipper delay. Keeping the setting foot suspended is what lets the whirl and the swirl run as a single movement rather than two.
source_note: Editorial: reworded so the whirl and swirl read as one integrated movement rather than a sequence.

new_value: The key is keeping the setting foot suspended so the whirl and the swirl stay part of one movement instead of separating into two. Holding that first leg up is what keeps the coordinated action smooth.
source_note: Editorial: reworded to describe one coordinated movement rather than two chained halves.

new_value: A whirl and a swirl are the two motions, with clipper delays on both feet tying them together. The new coordination is running both as one movement without letting them separate.
source_note: Editorial: reworded to avoid sequential framing.

new_value: Twirl is a swirling osis where the swirl is performed with the opposite leg from the osis. From a clipper delay, complete a cross-body around the world, and just as the leg heads back toward the ground, hop off the other leg and turn out of the move, scooping the bag into an osis.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: It is close to a dyno, with the difference that the swirl happens with the other foot. Eliminating any pause between the around the world and the turn into the osis is what makes it flow.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A reliable osis and a swirling around the world are the foundations, and a solid dyno is good preparation. The new element is splitting the swirl and the osis across opposite legs.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Flurry is a leg over straight after a barrage. From a clipper stall, plant quickly, hop off the opposite leg and bring it around the bag twice for the barrage, then hop off the first leg and bring the opposite leg over the top of the bag to land a toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The whole move hinges on flowing out of the barrage's two circles directly into the leg over without losing the bag's line. Keeping the barrage tight leaves time for the leg over at the end.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A solid barrage and a leg over are the two halves. The challenge is stringing the leg over immediately onto the back of the barrage.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Reverse magellan is an orbit followed by an around the world. From a toe delay, set and immediately orbit the bag with the setting foot, plant that leg, then bring it back up into an around the world, ending on a toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The move runs the two circles in the reverse of the usual order, so the trick is the quick plant and relift between them. Keeping the bag close keeps both circles small enough to chain.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with both an around the world and an orbit off a toe set is the core skill. The new piece is connecting the two opposite circles with a fast replant.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Quantum illusion plays like a miraging reverse mirage. From a toe delay, set the bag up and bring the opposite leg up and over into a mirage; plant that leg and quickly jump off the setting leg into a reverse mirage, catching on the opposite toe.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The two dexterities run in opposite directions back to back, so the timing of the plant between them is the key. A quick, vertical set gives both room to finish.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with mirage dexterities in both directions off a toe set is the foundation. The new demand is reversing the second dex relative to the first and linking them cleanly.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Da da curve is a mirage into a butterfly. From a clipper delay, bring the opposite leg over the bag by turning your hips for the mirage, and without putting that leg down, hop off the first leg while bringing it over the bag into a butterfly, catching in a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The move never sets the first dexing leg down, so keeping it suspended through the hip turn is what links the mirage into the butterfly. A controlled hip turn keeps the bag on line for the second circle.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A mirage and a butterfly are the two components. The new coordination is flowing from the mirage straight into the butterfly without resetting the leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Hop over swirl is a hopping-over around the world. Holding an inside delay close to the ground, the support leg jumps the suspended bag while the other leg sets it a few inches straight up and completes an around the world, ending in a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The defining move is the support leg hopping over the low bag while the other leg does the work, so a low, controlled set is essential. Keeping the set small, just a few inches, keeps the hop and the circle in sync.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A steady inside delay and an around the world are the foundations. The new element is the support leg hopping the bag during the move.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Barraging osis is a barrage into an osis. From a clipper delay, quickly plant the foot and hop off the opposite leg, bringing it around the bag twice for the barrage; as you complete the second circle, hop off the first foot and turn to catch the bag in an osis.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The hard part is turning into the osis right off the second barrage circle without a pause. Keeping the barrage tight leaves time and a clean line for the osis turn.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A solid barrage and a reliable osis are the two halves. The new coordination is folding the turn into the osis directly onto the end of the barrage.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Paradox symposium mirage sets from a clipper and switches the support leg, raising the opposite leg as you plant the setting foot. Then, without planting that opposite leg, hop off the setting leg and bring it up and over the bag into a mirage, ending on the opposite toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The support-leg switch is the distinctive moment, and keeping the raised leg up rather than planting it is what makes the symposium-style finish possible. A clean switch keeps the bag positioned for the over-the-top dex.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with paradox-style dexterities and a symposium mirage are the foundations. The new piece is switching the support leg and keeping it raised through the finish.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Tapping mirage taps the ground in the middle of the dexterity. From a toe delay, set the bag straight up, then quickly bring the opposite leg over the bag to tap the ground beside it, before bringing that same leg back over into a mirage, ending on a toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The ground tap between the two halves of the dexterity is the signature, so the timing of the tap has to fit inside the bag's hang time. A straight, tall set gives room for the tap and the return.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with mirage dexterities off a toe set is the foundation. The new element is the deliberate ground tap with the dexing foot partway through.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Symposium whirl is a whirl done without ever putting the setting leg down. From a clipper delay, jump hard off the opposite leg and bring it over the bag from the front, up and over, while keeping the setting leg raised, then land back on the opposite leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The one change from a plain whirl is keeping the setting leg up throughout, which makes the move a symposium variant. A hard jump off the opposite leg gives the height to circle the bag while balanced on one leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A solid whirl is the direct prerequisite. The new demand is the strength and balance to complete the whirl without setting the second leg down.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Paradox blur traces an S around the support leg. From a clipper stall, bring the setting leg around the support leg and around the bag to complete the S shape, then hop off the other leg and bring it around the bag, landing on a toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The S-shaped path of the first leg around the support leg is the defining motion and the part to drill. Keeping the bag close lets the second dex follow cleanly.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with paradox-style dexterities is the core skill. The new element is wrapping the first leg around the support leg to trace the S before the second dex.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Barfly swirl finishes a barfly with a swirl. From a clipper, set the bag around waist height travelling across your body, circle it twice with the setting leg for the barfly, then hop off the opposite leg and swirl the bag with it, ending in a clipper delay on that foot.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Setting the bag across the body sets up the double circle, and the swirl has to come right off the second one. A controlled cross-body set keeps the bag placed for both the barfly circles and the swirl.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A barfly and a swirl are the two halves. The new coordination is transitioning from the barfly's second circle straight into the swirl on the opposite leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Smudge is the reverse-dexterity version of smear. From a toe, dex the bag with the setting leg, then dex it back the other way with the opposite leg, and catch the bag on the toe.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: It mirrors smear by running the two dexterities in the opposite directions, so if smear is solid the main task is reversing each dex. Keeping both circles small and the bag central keeps the two halves connected.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with a pixie set and dexterities in both directions is the foundation, and smear is its direct counterpart. The new element is reversing the dex directions relative to smear.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Blizzard plays like a blurry reverse symposium mirage. From a clipper, set and do a quick blurry, then jump over the bag with the setting leg and complete the reverse dexterity without planting the opposite foot, ending on the opposite toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Linking the blurry directly into the over-the-bag dexterity is the hard part, and keeping the opposite foot from planting is what gives it the symposium feel. A quick, controlled blurry leaves time for the finish.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A blurry and a symposium mirage are the foundations. The new coordination is chaining the blurry into the reversed over-the-top dex without resetting.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Quantum mirage is two consecutive mirages. From a toe set, do a mirage with one leg, then a mirage with the other leg, catching the bag on the opposite toe.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Both dexterities are mirages run back to back, so the rhythm of switching legs while keeping each circle clean is the key. A tall, central set gives both room to finish.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with mirage dexterities off a toe set is the core skill. The new element is stacking two of them on alternating legs in a row.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Gyro butterfly swirl is a gyrating butterfly into a swirl. From a clipper set somewhat behind you, turn passing the bag behind your back and bring the setting leg over the bag in a butterfly motion without ever putting it down, then land on that leg as you complete a swirl with the other leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The gyro entry, letting the bag pass behind the back, sets up the butterfly without a plant, and the swirl comes off the landing. Keeping the setting leg up through the butterfly is what chains it into the swirl.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A gyro butterfly and a swirl are the two halves. The new coordination is flowing from the no-plant butterfly directly into the swirl on the other leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pogo paradox mirage uses a pogo hop into a mirage. From a clipper, set the bag around waist height and, without putting the setting leg down, hop over the bag with the other leg; then bring the setting leg around the bag into a mirage, land on it, and catch the bag on the opposite toe.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The pogo hop keeps the setting leg up so the mirage follows without a plant. A waist-height set gives time for the hop and the mirage in sequence.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Comfort with a pogo hop and a paradox mirage are the foundations. The new element is keeping the setting leg up through the hop before the mirage.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Refraction is an inside delay carried through a full spin. Start by inside-delaying the bag, then complete a 360-degree spin toward the support leg while wrapping the bag, keeping it on the foot throughout.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Eliminating any pause between the initial inside delay and the spin is what makes it look smooth. The wrap holds the bag in place as you turn.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A reliable inside delay and comfort spinning while keeping the bag on the foot are the foundations. The new element is carrying the delay through a full wrapping spin.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pogo barfly uses a pogo hop into a barfly. From a clipper delay, hop over the bag with the opposite leg without putting the clipper down, then circle the bag twice with the setting leg for the barfly, ending on a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The pogo hop keeps the clipper foot up so the barfly's two circles follow without a plant. A controlled hop sets the bag up for the double circle.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A pogo hop and a barfly are the two halves. The new element is launching the barfly straight off the hop without planting.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pixie swirl is a pixie set into a swirl. From a toe set, circle the bag with the setting leg for the pixie, then hop to the side and swirl with that leg, coming from the back up and over the bag, landing on a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The pixie set has to be clean and the hop sets up the swirl coming from behind. A tight pixie keeps the bag placed for the over-the-top swirl.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A pixie set and a swirl are the two halves. The new coordination is hopping out of the pixie into the back-to-front swirl.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Double over down swirl is a double over down finished with a swirl. From a toe set, circle the bag twice with the setting leg for the double over down, then perform a swirl with the opposite leg, ending in a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The double over down has to stay tight so the swirl can come off the second circle cleanly. Reading the move as the over-down first, then the swirl, keeps the timing manageable.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A double over down and a swirl are the two halves. The new coordination is transitioning from the second over-down circle into the swirl on the opposite leg.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pixie same side illusion runs a pixie into an illusion. From a toe, do a quick pixie dexterity and touch the ground with that foot, then come back around the bag into an illusion, ending on the opposite toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Touching the ground between the pixie and the illusion resets the foot for the reverse circle. A clean, quick pixie keeps the bag placed for the illusion.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A pixie set and an illusion are the two halves. The new element is the ground touch that links the pixie into the reverse-direction illusion.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Paradon swirl is a paradon finished with a swirl. From a toe set, circle the bag twice with the opposite leg for the paradon, then perform a swirl with the setting leg, ending in a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The paradon's two circles have to stay tight so the swirl comes off the second cleanly. Treating it as the paradon first, then the swirl, keeps the timing clear.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A paradon and a swirl are the two halves. The new coordination is transitioning from the paradon's second circle into the swirl.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pogo paradox barrage uses a pogo hop into a barrage. From a clipper, hop over the bag with the opposite leg without putting the setting leg down, then circle the bag twice with the setting leg for the barrage, ending on a toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The pogo hop keeps the setting leg up so the barrage's two circles follow without a plant. A controlled hop sets up the double circle.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A pogo hop and a barrage are the two halves. The new element is launching the barrage off the hop without planting.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: High plains drifter circles twice into a clipper delay. From a clipper delay, quickly plant and circle the bag twice with the opposite leg, then delay the bag on that clipper.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The quick plant frees the opposite leg for the double circle, and keeping it tight lands the clipper cleanly. Speed on the two circles is the main demand.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A clipper delay and comfort double-circling the bag are the foundations. The new element is the fast plant-and-double-circle into the opposite clipper.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Gyro butterfly is a butterfly entered through a gyro. From a clipper, let the bag pass behind your back and, without putting the setting leg down, turn and complete a butterfly delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The gyro, letting the bag travel behind the back, sets up the butterfly without a plant. Keeping the setting leg up through the turn is what makes it a gyro butterfly rather than a plain butterfly.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A butterfly and comfort letting the bag pass behind the back are the foundations. The new element is completing the butterfly off the gyro without planting.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Fairy clipper is a fairy set caught on a clipper. From a toe set, quickly circle the bag with the setting foot for the fairy, then catch the bag on that same foot in a clipper.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A clean, quick fairy circle keeps the bag placed for the clipper catch on the same foot. The whole move is the fairy circle resolving straight onto a clipper.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A fairy set and a clipper delay are the two halves. The new element is catching the fairy on the same-foot clipper.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Paradox barrage circles twice into a toe delay. From a clipper delay, circle the bag twice with the setting foot for the barrage, then end on the opposite toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The two circles have to stay tight and quick to reach the opposite toe cleanly. Keeping the bag close keeps both circles small.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A clipper delay and comfort double-circling the bag are the foundations. The new element is doubling the circle straight into a toe catch.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Double fairy circles the fairy dex twice. From a toe set, circle the bag twice with the setting foot for the fairy, ending on the opposite toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The two fairy circles have to stay tight and quick to reach the opposite toe. Keeping the bag close keeps both small.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A fairy set is the foundation. The new element is doubling the fairy circle into a toe catch.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Pogo paradox eggbeater runs two dexterities off a pogo set. Do a quick pogo set, then dex with one leg and dex with the other, ending on a toe delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Both dexterities come quickly off the pogo set, so keeping the pogo tight and the two dexes fast is the key. A clean pogo keeps the bag placed for both.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A pogo set and comfort with consecutive dexterities are the foundations. The new element is stacking the two dexes straight off the pogo.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Double whirl circles twice in the whirl direction. From a clipper set, circle the bag twice in the whirling direction, returning to a clipper delay.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The two whirl circles have to flow without a pause to land back on the clipper. Keeping the rhythm even across both circles is the key.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A whirl is the foundation. The new element is doubling the whirl circle before the catch.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Tombstone is a high plains drifter with a plant added after the first dexterity. Run the drifter, but plant the foot after the first dex before completing the move.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The plant after the first dex is the only change from a high plains drifter. A solid drifter underneath makes the plant the single new element.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A high plains drifter is the direct prerequisite. The new element is the plant inserted after the first dexterity.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Stepping rev whirl is a stepping set into a reverse whirl on the opposite side of the body. Take the stepping set, then complete a reverse whirl with the bag travelling to the opposite side.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The stepping set has to place the bag across the body so the reverse whirl finishes on the opposite side. A clean stepping set is what sets up the opposite-side finish.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A stepping set and a reverse whirl are the two halves. The new element is directing the reverse whirl to the opposite side off the stepping set.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: Grave digger is a barrage into an osis with a plant added between its two dexterities. Run the barrage into the osis, but plant the foot between the two dexterities.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: The plant between the dexes is the only change from the plain barrage-into-osis. A solid version underneath makes the inserted plant the single new element.
source_note: Extracted from the legacy footbag.org move description into structured instructional prose.

new_value: A barrage into an osis is the direct prerequisite. The new element is the plant between the two dexterities.
