new_value: Perform a full around-the-world (a complete circle of the leg around the bag in-out) but instead of finishing on the usual delay, bring the leg back under and catch the bag on the heel. It is a standard leg circle terminated on the heel surface.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The heel catch is what makes this hard: the heel is a small, less forgiving surface, so keep the set high enough that the circle finishes cleanly and the heel arrives flat and level under the bag as it drops.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable around-the-world and a comfortable heel delay on its own. This variant only swaps the terminal surface onto that circle.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag straight up and pass the free leg outward under it for a clean uptime dexterity, then carry that same leg across the body to catch the bag on a clipper stall. The atomic is that outward uptime dex added ahead of the cross-body clipper catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Let the outward dexterity fully clear before you swing across to the clipper; crowding the two together is what turns a clean atomic into a scrappy cross-body catch. Keep the set high and vertical so there is room for both.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable clipper stall and a comfortable outward uptime dexterity. Atomic just adds that out-dex ahead of the cross-body catch you already own.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe set, perform the butterfly leg-over out-in into a cross-body clipper delay, but keep the dexterity pinned to the same side as the plant leg instead of bringing the free leg across from the opposite side. It is the ordinary butterfly wing, only worked on the plant side.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Same-side is the more cramped of the two orientations, since the leg-over has to clear on the same side it is planting. Give the set enough height for the wing to complete before you settle onto the clipper.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable standard butterfly and its clipper catch; same-side only changes which side the leg-over dexterity runs on, so own the base first.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set the bag up, bring the setting leg across your body, and rotate the foot so the sole turns inward, then catch and stall the bag on the top of the foot held across you. The across-body position with the inward-turned sole is what makes it a clipper rather than a plain toe delay.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Get the foot fully across the body and flat before the bag arrives; if the sole is not turned inward the bag tends to glance off the outside of the foot instead of settling into the stall.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A steady toe or foot delay. The clipper adds only the across-body turn to a stall you can already hold.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Take a guay (a pickup into a leg dexterity that lands on an inside stall) but carry the leg out-in rather than in-out, and let the bag pass up and over the body during the dexterity before it settles on the same-side inside delay. The dive is the bag travelling over you, added onto that reversed guay dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Give the pickup enough height for the bag to clear over the body and still drop cleanly onto the inside stall; rushing the reversed dexterity is what usually costs the catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable guay landing cleanly on the inside stall, plus comfort sending the bag up and over the body in a dive. This only adds the over-the-body pass and the reversed direction to that dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and circle the setting leg out-in around it twice in one continuous motion, then recatch on the setting toe stall. It is two stacked illusion dexterities feeding a single set.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set high and keep both out-in passes flowing without a plant between them; the second dexterity has to start before the first one has finished, or the bag drops before the opposite-side catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable single illusion on both sides. Double illusion only chains a second out-in dexterity onto that same set before the recatch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set the bag up, jump off the ground, and contact it with both feet at once before landing. It is the foot analogue of a double-knee: two simultaneous surfaces meeting the bag in the air.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The hard part is timing the jump so both feet arrive on the bag together and symmetrically; a set that is too low leaves no airtime, and one foot leading turns it into a single contact.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Be comfortable leaving the ground for a contact and reading a vertical set; a solid single-foot air contact is the piece you are doubling up.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag high and dip your head and upper body beneath it, then carry the opposite leg over the bag twice (in-out then out-in) and recatch on a same-side toe delay. The duck under the bag is the only thing ducking adds to a plain double-leg-over.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Give the set enough height and hang time to fit the duck and both leg-overs; it usually falls apart when the set is too low and the duck collides with the dexterities.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable double-leg-over on both sides, plus a comfortable duck under the bag. Layer the ducking in only once the double dexterity is automatic.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag and pass your head beneath it as it travels, then swing the opposite leg through an inside dexterity and recatch on a same-side inside stall. Ducking under the bag is what turns a plain guay into a ducking guay.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Time the duck to the bag's flight: you pass under it while it is up, as one motion with the dexterity that follows; ducking too early or too late pulls you off the inside catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable guay (the inside dexterity into a same-side inside stall) and a comfortable duck under the bag on a simpler base before you combine the two.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and drop your body down low beneath the flight, then rise back up and recatch on a toe delay on the same surface. The body-dip under the set is what makes it a ducking move rather than a plain set-and-catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set high enough that there is real time to sink and recover; the duck is a genuine drop of the body under the bag, not a token bob of the head.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable toe stall and comfort with a controlled high set. Ducking only adds the body-dip beneath that set.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a butterfly, but pin the dexterity to the opposite side: set the bag up and reach the far leg across your body to clip through the butterfly and recatch, instead of working the near leg. Driving the dexterity with the opposite-side leg is what makes it a far butterfly.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The reach across the body is the hard part. Angle the set slightly toward the far side and open the hips so the opposite leg can clear the bag cleanly. Falling short on that reach is the usual miss.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable butterfly on the near side. Far only moves that same dexterity onto the opposite-side leg.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, spin behind your back through the rotation and, without pausing, run the flurry's rapid string of leg dexterities out of the turn, recatching on a toe delay. The full behind-the-back rotation is the gyro carried onto the flurry.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Get the whole gyro rotation done up front so the bag comes out clean into the flurry; if you are still turning when the first dexterity fires, the string of moves crowds together and collapses.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable flurry and a comfortable gyro on their own. Flurricane only wraps the behind-the-back rotation around a flurry you can already run cleanly.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, drop a quick plant on the setting side and send the free leg through an in-out dexterity; then carry the setting leg into a whirling motion, and as it passes over the bag turn into the spin and recatch on an osis on that same leg.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Time the turn to the moment the whirling leg is over the bag. Commit the rotation there and let it flow straight into the osis, rather than finishing the whirl first and then trying to spin into the catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable blender and a comfortable osis catch. Food processor layers the turning whirl-to-osis finish onto that base.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set for a double-over-down and, as the bag descends, layer a reverse mirage into it: sweep the free leg out-in over the dropping bag through the double legover before the down catch. The out-in dexterity carried onto the descending double-down is what turns it into a fusion.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the double legover and the out-in sweep as one flowing motion into the down catch; the trick usually breaks when you finish the double-over first and then reach for the mirage as a separate step.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable double-over-down and a clean reverse mirage on their own. Fusion only adds the out-in dexterity onto the down move you already own.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, turn through a full back spin, then carry the setting leg back over the bag in-out through a dexterity and recatch on a same-side inside stall. The gyro is that added body rotation threaded onto the guay's dexterity into an inside stall.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Ride the rotation straight into the in-out dexterity without a pause; set high enough to finish the spin, find the leg for the in-out, and settle the inside stall in one flow.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable guay and a comfortable gyro. Spin the clipper set cleanly on its own before threading the in-out dexterity and inside stall onto it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, spin a full rotation and, without pausing, carry the setting leg over the bag out-in and recatch on a toe delay. The spin folded into the leg-over dexterity is what turns a plain legover into a gyro legover.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The spin and the leg-over are one continuous move: pausing to find the bag after the rotation is where it usually breaks down, so set high enough to give the spin time and let the setting leg clear the bag on the way over.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable legover and a comfortable gyro on their own. Land the leg-over dexterity cleanly before you try to carry a full rotation into it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, spin behind your back through a full rotation, then send the same leg in-out under the bag and recatch on a same-side toe delay. The added back-spin is the gyro laid onto an ordinary pickup dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the rotation and the in-out dexterity as one flowing move; if you finish the spin before starting the pickup you will run out of time and reach for the toe delay. Set high enough to complete the turn cleanly.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable pickup on both sides and a comfortable gyro spin. Land each on its own before combining them.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and spin a full rotation behind your back, then meet the bag on the same-side toe delay as you come around. The complete body turn under the set is the gyro.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set high enough to give the whole rotation time to finish, and pick the bag back up with your eyes early so you can settle it onto the toe the instant you come around. Rushing the spin and looking late is where the recatch is lost.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A steady toe delay and a comfortable free-standing gyro. Land the spin back onto the toe on its own before stacking it on a set.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, spin backward through the set and flow straight into the whirling-swirl's dexterities: carry the setting leg through in-out, then take the opposite leg through a back swirl, and recatch cross-body on a clipper delay. The backward rotation is a gyro layered onto the whirling-swirl.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Let the backspin drive the whole move rather than treating it as a separate step before the swirl; if the rotation stalls, the opposite-leg back swirl runs out of room and the cross-body recatch collapses.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable whirling-swirl and a comfortable gyro. Own the backspin on its own before threading it through the swirl dexterities.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and spin in-out through a full body rotation, then catch the bag cross-body on a clipper delay. The in-out spin carried onto the clipper is what makes this an inspinning clipper rather than a plain clipper.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Let the rotation finish before the bag arrives so the free leg is already crossed into clipper position; reaching for the clipper before the spin completes is where it usually comes apart.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable clipper stall and a comfortable inspinning rotation on their own. Inspinning only adds the in-out body spin to that stall.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag high and spin into the set, then carry the free leg over the bag out-in and recatch on a toe delay. The inspin folds a full body rotation into the illusion so you meet the bag already turned.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set higher than a plain illusion to buy time for the rotation; the spin has to finish just as the leg comes over, not before you have found the bag again.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable illusion on both sides and a controlled inspin. Land the rotation and the out-in dexterity on their own before combining them.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and drive into an inward spin as you carry the free leg over the bag, then recatch on a toe delay. The in-direction body rotation folded into the legover is what separates it from a plain legover.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Commit to the spin as the leg comes over and run the rotation and the legover as one motion; hesitating mid-spin is what pulls the bag out of line for the recatch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable legover and a comfortable inward spin on their own. Inspinning only adds the in-direction rotation to the dexterity you already have.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and carry a full inward spin under it, then take the free leg over the bag on the in dexterity and recatch on a toe delay. It is a standard mirage with an inspin folded into the set.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Finish the inspin and get squared up before the free leg goes over the bag; trying to complete the dexterity while still rotating is where the mirage falls apart.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable mirage on both sides and a clean inspin on its own. Inspinning only threads that spin into the dexterity you already have.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Enter a reverse guay by leading with an inside spin: let the inspinning rotation turn your body into the trick so the reversed set and delay come out of the spin rather than from a square stance. The inspin is the entry; the reverse guay itself runs unchanged once you have come around.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Commit to the full inspinning rotation before the bag arrives. Rushing the spin so you meet the set only half-turned is where this usually collapses. The rotation should deliver you cleanly into the reverse guay, not fight it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable reverse guay and a comfortable inspinning entry on their own. This trick only feeds the guay out of an inside spin, so both halves should be solid before you combine them.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag up and turn through a full in-out spin, then come back around to recatch the bag on a toe delay. The inward rotation under the set is what makes it an inspinning stall rather than a plain toe-set-and-catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Give yourself a clean, slightly forward set so you have room to complete the turn and still meet the bag; the usual failure is under-rotating or drifting off the bag before the toe delay arrives.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable toe delay on both sides and a controlled in-out spin. Land the rotation on its own before adding the toe-stall catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a paradox whirl, but carry a full spin through the move while ducking your upper body under the bag as you pass through the dexterity. The gyro and the dip are both layered onto the paradox whirl's motion, all resolved before the recatch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Give yourself a higher, cleaner set than a plain paradox whirl needs: the added spin and the duck each cost time, and rushing either one is what collapses the base move underneath them.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable paradox whirl on its own, plus comfort adding a spin and ducking under a set. Stack the body movement on only once the base move is automatic.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a set roughly waist height, circle the free leg around the bag in-out once for the miraging dexterity, then immediately carry the same leg in-out a second time for the pickup itself, and recatch on a toe delay. It reads as a doubled in-out around a single set, the miraging simply adding the leading circle in front of the pickup's own dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set a touch higher than a plain pickup and keep the two circles as one flowing motion off that single set; the move usually breaks down when the set is too low to fit both dexterities or when you pause between the first and second circle.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable pickup and a comfortable mirage-style free-leg circle on both sides. Miraging only stacks a second in-out dexterity ahead of the pickup you already know.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, turn so the bag passes behind your back and, without planting the setting foot, spin through into a leg dexterity, catching on an osis. The full behind-the-back rotation is the gyro carried onto a torque.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The spin and the dexterity are one continuous move; pausing between the rotation and the catch is where it usually falls apart.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A clean torque and a comfortable gyro. Land the osis on its own before adding the rotation.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Run the paradox-symposium entry as on the standard chassis (a symposium set carrying a paradox spin) but instead of closing with a single mirage, ride the momentum straight into the eggbeater's paired out-in dexterities and recatch on a delay. The spin lives inside the symposium set, and the two-dexterity eggbeater terminal simply replaces the lone mirage.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the set tall enough to survive the paradox spin, because both eggbeater dexterities have to happen in one continuous flow: the usual failure is running out of height after the spin so the second dexterity has no room to complete.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Be comfortable with the base symposium-eggbeater and with the paradox spin on its own; landing a clean paradox-symposium-mirage first helps, since the eggbeater only extends that same chassis at its terminal.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a legover, add a full spin so you rotate through the leg-over rather than taking it stationary: set the bag, carry the free leg over it, and let a gyro turn your body all the way around before you recatch. The rotation carried onto the legover is what makes it a parallax rather than a plain legover.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Treat the spin and the leg pass as one motion: the gyro should turn under the bag, not interrupt it; pausing to complete the leg-over before the spin (or vice versa) is where it usually breaks down.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable legover on both sides and a comfortable gyro on their own. Land each cleanly before layering the spin onto the leg-over.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a single set, take the free leg through a same-side in-out dexterity, then flow straight into the barrage (an opposite-side in-out dexterity followed by another same-side in-out) and recatch on a delay. It is three consecutive dexterities carried on one set with no re-set between them.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Everything rides on that one set, so it has to go up clean and high enough to fit all three dexterities; the middle dexterity is where it collapses when the set is rushed or too low.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable barrage (its opposite-in, same-in dexterity pair) plus a comfortable pixie dexterity; build the two-dex barrage first, then add the leading pixie dex.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Open with a pixie launch (a toe set that carries the same-side leg inward as it lifts the bag) and deliver it up into a cross-body clipper stall. The pixie only governs how the bag is launched; the trick still finishes as a clean clipper catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the inward dexterity of the pixie set tidy so the bag rises straight and on line; when the launch drifts sideways, the cross-body clipper has nowhere consistent to meet it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable clipper stall on its own, plus comfort with the pixie's toe set and its same-side inward dexterity. Pixie only changes how the clipper is entered, not the catch itself.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, open with the quantum (a quick, compressed cross-body toe launch) then circle the free leg in-out around the bag and pick it up from below onto a toe delay. The quantum is that tight launch stacked ahead of the pickup's natural in-out dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the quantum tight and quick; its whole character is a compact cross-body launch, so if it opens wide and slow there is no time to bring the free leg back and scoop the pickup from below.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable pickup (the in-out dexterity caught from below on a toe delay) done automatically, plus comfort with the compressed quantum toe launch that opens it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a swirl exactly as usual, but reverse the direction the free leg carries through the dexterity. The set and the catch stay the same; only the path of the dexterity flips to the opposite direction.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The reversal is the whole challenge: the reversed dexterity feels foreign even off a solid swirl, so groove the new direction slowly before bringing it up to speed.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable swirl. Rev swirl only reverses the direction of that same dexterity, so own the forward version on both sides first.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a whirl but circle the bag the opposite way: drive the dexterity from the knee, letting the ankle loop around the bag in the reversed direction before recatching. The whole motion still lives in the knee and ankle, only the circling direction flips.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the circle powered by the knee with the ankle tracing the bag; if you let the hip swing the upper leg you have drifted into a butterfly instead. Reversing the direction is the only change, so let the knee-driven feel of the whirl carry over.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable whirl, since the ankle circling the bag off the knee is the whole foundation; rev whirl only reverses the direction of that dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reverse drifter is a standard drifter run in the opposite direction: the same dexterity and catch, but the move is carried the mirrored way across the body. Groove the drifter first, then drive that identical motion in the reversed direction.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The direction flip is the only thing that changes, so the trick lives or dies on how cleanly you can carry the drifter's dexterity in the less-familiar direction. Drill the base drifter both ways before treating the reverse as its own move.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable drifter in the standard direction; reverse drifter only flips the direction of that same move, so the base has to be solid first.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, hold the cross-body position and circle the setting leg twice around the footbag in either direction, then recatch on a clipper delay on the same side. It is simply a swirl doubled: two full revolutions instead of one before the catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Give the bag enough height and hang time to fit both circles in; the usual failure is running out of air on the second revolution and rushing the catch. Keep the two circles continuous and evenly paced rather than snatching the second one.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable single swirl and a solid clipper delay on the same side. Ripstein only adds a second revolution to a swirl you can already land.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a down-double-down, but carry a full spin through the move so the rotation folds into the two down catches instead of being tacked on. The spin is woven into the existing dexterities rather than turned as a separate beat.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Let the spin drive the timing rather than fighting it; the common failure is completing the rotation and the down-double-down as two separate beats instead of one continuous move.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable down-double-down on both sides plus a controlled spin on its own. Land each independently before folding the spin into the base.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, hop off the free leg and swing it around the bag out-in to plant and take your weight, then bring the setting leg over the bag in-out into the legover catch. Scrambled means the second dexterity travels opposite in direction to the first.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The free leg has to fully land and support your weight before the setting leg comes over; rushing that plant is the usual failure point, since the two dexterities run in opposite directions and cannot be hurried into one another.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A clean legover and comfort miraging in both directions. Scrambled only reverses the second dexterity, so you need each direction reliable on its own first.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, pixie the bag straight up and circle it in-out; hop off the free leg and carry it around the bag in-out, then hop off the setting leg and recatch a toe delay on that same setting leg. Hopping off both legs is what separates a smear from a grounded mirage.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the pixie set low and centered over you so both hops stay controlled; rushing the second hop off the setting leg is where the recatch usually gets lost.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable mirage and a comfortable pixie set. The smear just adds the hop off both legs to that in-out dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a butterfly but carry a full spin through the dexterity, and instead of settling the bag into a delay at the end, finish by kicking it back up. The spin is layered onto the butterfly and the closing kick replaces the delay.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Complete the rotation just as the free leg comes to meet the bag so the kick launches cleanly; rushing into the kick before the spin finishes is what throws the bag off-line.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable butterfly kick and a controlled spin on its own. This only adds the full rotation to a butterfly you can already finish with a kick.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a guay (the same-side dexterity that recovers onto an inside stall) but carry a full spin through the move, letting the rotation turn you all the way around before the bag settles on the same-side inside stall. The spin is woven into the dexterity rather than added before or after it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the rotation and the catch as one continuous motion and spot the bag as you come around, so the inside stall arrives under control instead of being chased after the spin finishes.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable guay on both sides and a comfortable free spin. Land the inside-stall recovery cleanly on its own before folding in the full rotation.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, spin your body all the way around as you carry the free leg over the bag out-in, then recatch on a toe delay. The added full rotation is what turns a plain illusion into a spinning illusion.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The spin and the out-in dexterity are one continuous motion, not two steps. Set high enough to complete the rotation before the toe catch, which is where it usually breaks down.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable illusion on both sides and a comfortable full body spin. Land the illusion cleanly on its own before layering the full spin on top.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, turn into a full back spin and carry the free leg over the bag out-in as you come around, catching on a same-side toe delay. The rotation is a whole-body spin folded directly into an ordinary legover dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The spin and the legover are one motion, not two: riding the rotation all the way through before sending the leg over is what usually goes wrong; set high enough that the full turn has time to finish under the bag.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable legover and a clean full body spin on both sides. Land each one on its own before joining the spin to the dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, spin the body all the way around and, as you come out of the rotation, carry the free leg in-out over the bag and recatch on a toe delay. The full body spin folded into a plain mirage dexterity is what makes it spinning.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set high enough that the spin finishes before the leg comes over: the rotation and the mirage have to read as one continuous move, not a spin followed by a separate catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable mirage on both sides and a comfortable spin on its own; spinning only wraps a full body rotation around a dexterity you already own.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set the bag up with enough height to clear your body, spin all the way around, and recatch the descending bag on a toe stall as you come out of the rotation. The full body spin between the set and the toe catch is what makes this a spinning toe stall rather than a plain set-and-stall.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Give the set enough height and hang time to complete the spin before the bag arrives, and pick the bag back up with your eyes the instant you come around so the toe is already reaching for it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable toe stall and a comfortable full body spin on their own. This trick only adds the rotation between the set and the catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Spin through the gyro, but instead of dexing straight out of the rotation, plant the dexing foot down first and then carry it through the dexterity. That brief plant ahead of the dex is what separates a spyro from an ordinary gyro.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Let the foot actually set down before the dex: rushing from the spin straight into the dexterity collapses it back into a plain gyro, so give the plant its own beat.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A comfortable gyro and the dexterity you are spinning into; spyro only adds the planted step before the dex to that foundation.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Open with the stepping entry (carry the leading leg over the bag in-out) then spin behind your back and recatch on a cross-body clipper. The step-over is stacked ahead of the osis so the leading dexterity feeds straight into the spin.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the stepping leg-over feeding continuously into the osis spin; hesitating between the step and the rotation is where it usually falls apart.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable osis and a comfortable stepping motion on its own before joining the two. Stepping only prefaces the osis with that leading dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Open with the stepping entry (carry the leading leg over the bag) then flow straight into the pickup's dexterity and recatch on a toe delay. The step-over is stacked ahead of the normal pickup so the two dexterities run back-to-back into the toe terminal.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the stepping leg-over feeding continuously into the pickup dex so the whole thing reads as one motion; hesitating between the step and the pickup is where it usually falls apart.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable pickup that lands cleanly on the toe delay, plus a comfortable stepping motion on its own before joining the two.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag and carry the same leg in-out around it into the dexterity, catching back on the delay, but never plant the setting foot, keeping it off the ground through the whole move. Holding the support leg airborne is what turns a plain pixie into a symposium pixie.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The whole pixie has to happen while you balance on the raised setting leg; the usual failure is touching that foot down to steady the in-out dexterity, which quietly drops the symposium. Set a touch taller so there is time to finish the dex on one leg.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable pixie on both sides, plus comfort holding your balance with the setting foot off the ground. Symposium only adds the no-plant discipline to that set.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag straight up and carry the free leg over it out-in to lightly tap the ground beside the bag, then bring the same leg back over in-out and recatch on a toe delay. The ground-touch taken mid-dexterity is what turns a plain mirage into a tap.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the set low and vertical so there is time to touch down and get the leg back over the bag; the tap is a brush of the ground, not a weighted plant.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable mirage on both sides. Tap only adds the ground-touch to that dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a toe delay, set the bag straight up and carry the free leg over it out-in to lightly tap the ground beside the bag, then swing the same leg back in-out and recatch across your body on a clipper. The ground-tap during the dexterity is what makes it a tapping clipper rather than a plain cross-body catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the set low and vertical so there is time to brush the ground and still bring the leg across for the clipper; the tap is a light touch, not a weighted plant.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable clipper and a reliable ground-tap on both sides. This only adds the touch-down to a cross-body dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a legover set, carry the free leg over the bag and lightly tap the ground beside it, then bring the same leg back over and recatch on the same toe delay you would use for a plain legover. That ground-tap woven into the leg-over dexterity is what makes it a tapping legover.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the set low and vertical so there is time to touch down and recover before the recatch; the tap is a light brush of the ground, not a weighted plant that stalls the move.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable legover on both sides, since tapping only adds the ground-touch to that same dexterity. Get comfortable with the plain leg-over recatch first.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From the set, lead with the tapping dexterity (carry the free leg over the bag out-in and lightly brush the ground beside it) then complete the pickup's in-out dexterity and recatch on a toe delay on the same side. The leading ground-tap is what turns a plain pickup into a tapping pickup.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the tap a light brush during the leading dexterity, not a weighted plant; drop your weight onto the free foot and you lose the pickup's in-out that still has to follow.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable pickup on both sides and a comfortable tapping ground-touch. Tapping only adds the leading ground-brush ahead of that dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Perform a reverse guay and, during the setting motion, carry the free leg down to lightly tap the ground beside the bag before completing the set and recatching. The added ground-tap is what turns the reverse guay into a tapping variation.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the tap a light brush of the ground rather than a weighted plant, so the reverse guay's timing stays intact and there is time to recover into the catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A dependable reverse guay on both sides plus a comfortable ground-tap. Tapping only layers the touch-down onto the guay you already have.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Set the bag high and vertical, then turn through three complete 360 spins before it drops and recatch on the next kick. It is the single spin stacked three times onto one set, so each rotation has to be fast and tight to fit the airtime.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: The move lives or dies on the height and verticality of the set: give yourself enough airtime for three full turns and keep each spin compact so you come back around square to the bag.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable single and double spin plus a high, clean set. Triple spin only adds another rotation onto airtime you can already control.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: From a clipper set, let the bag pass behind your back and, without planting the setting foot, turn through a full rotation and complete a drifter. The behind-the-back spin is a gyro carried onto the drifter dexterity.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: Keep the rotation and the drifter as one continuous move; the spin has to stay tight enough that the setting foot never comes down before you finish the catch.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.

new_value: A reliable drifter and a comfortable gyro. Land the drifter cleanly on its own before wrapping the behind-the-back spin around it.
source_note: Phase B Technique Notes: authored from footbag.org description / settled decomposition; workflow + consistency-editor pass.
